steps.title: Scan the table for the measure where tribal activity stands out
steps.content: Tribal economic activity has the fewest employees (51,674) but generates \$7.3 billion in revenue—ranking between accommodation/food services (\$5.2B) and retail (\$10.7B) in total. The distinctive column is average contribution per employee: tribal activity reaches \$141,510, far exceeding retail (\$59,924) and accommodation/food services (\$34,861).

steps.title: Match this standout measure to the choices
steps.content: Choice C directly captures this unique advantage: tribal economic activity generates the highest revenue per worker. Choices A and B misread the data—tribal ranks middle in total contribution and lowest in raw employment, not highest in any single measure. Choice D contradicts the table (51,674 ≠ 150,373). That matches choice C — C is correct.
